name: Restaurant Gary Danko
notes: Grand Award winner from Wine Spectator with 2,600 wine selections. Aaron Babcock's program includes 30+ selections from Domaine de la Romanée-Conti and century-old Madeira by the glass.
source: Wine Spectator Restaurant Award (Grand Award), OpenTable reviews
accolades: Grand Award (Wine Spectator), Five Diamond (AAA), Relais & Châteaux

name: Acquerello
notes: Grand Award winner housed in former chapel. 2,300+ selections focusing on Italian wines, particularly Piedmont and Tuscany. Gianpaolo Paterlini oversees encyclopedic collection.
source: Wine Spectator Restaurant Award (Grand Award), Michelin Guide (2 stars)

name: Spruce
notes: Grand Award winner with 2,600-label program managed by Jaime Pinedo. 14,000 bottles in cellar, many available in large-format and half-bottles. Strong California and French focus.
source: Wine Spectator Restaurant Award (Grand Award), Bacchus Management Group

visits.notes: Outstanding wine service and generous pours

name: Lazy Bear
notes: Grand Award winner focusing on California wine with 2,300 selections. Jacob Brown's 'Field Guide to Wines' features older vintages from Beaulieu, Inglenook, Ridge dating to 1960s.

visits.notes: Communal dining with exceptional wine pairings

name: Saison
notes: Grand Award winner with 2,505 labels. Co-founder Mark Bright is also winemaker at Saison Winery. Strong Bordeaux, Burgundy, California, and Rhône Valley selections.
source: Wine Spectator Restaurant Award (Grand Award), Michelin Guide (2 stars)

visits.notes: Precise pours in fine dining setting

name: Absinthe Brasserie & Bar
notes: Best of Award of Excellence winner with 2,500 selections and 13,000-bottle inventory. Wine director Jack Hyland focuses on California, France, and Italy. Traditional French brasserie.
source: Wine Spectator Restaurant Award (Best of Award of Excellence)
accolades: Best of Award of Excellence (Wine Spectator)

visits.notes: Classic French atmosphere with solid wine service

name: A16
notes: Best of Award of Excellence winner focusing on Southern Italian wines. Shelly Lindgren's 4,000-bottle cellar emphasizes Campania, Sicily, and other Southern Italian regions.
source: Wine Spectator Restaurant Award (Best of Award of Excellence)
accolades: Best of Award of Excellence (Wine Spectator)

visits.notes: Excellent Italian wine selection, generous pours

name: Quince
notes: Best of Award of Excellence winner in historic Jackson Square. Wine director Adam Chhibbane curates 1,700 labels including aged Beaujolais, skin-contact rosés, and biodynamic Burgundies.
source: Wine Spectator Restaurant Award (Best of Award of Excellence), Michelin Guide (1 star)
accolades: Best of Award of Excellence (Wine Spectator), 1 Michelin Star

visits.notes: Sophisticated wine program in elegant setting

name: Sons & Daughters
notes: Best of Award of Excellence winner (2024) with Nordic-inspired cuisine. Wine director David Kolvek features selections from Burgundy, California, and Italy including Domaine Roulot and Littorai.
source: Wine Spectator Restaurant Award (Best of Award of Excellence 2024)
accolades: Best of Award of Excellence (Wine Spectator)

visits.notes: Contemporary dining with thoughtful wine pairings

name: Californios
notes: Best of Award of Excellence winner highlighting California and Mexican producers. Lead sommelier Ameena Elmore manages 900 labels including Valle de Guadalupe and Napa selections.
source: Wine Spectator Restaurant Award (Best of Award of Excellence), Michelin Guide (2 stars)
accolades: Best of Award of Excellence (Wine Spectator), 2 Michelin Stars

visits.notes: Unique wine program featuring Mexican producers

name: Kitchen Istanbul
notes: New Best of Award of Excellence winner (2024) with 370 selections from 1,500-bottle cellar. Wine director Joseph DiGrigoli focuses on France, Italy, with gems from Piedmont and Rhône Valley.
source: Wine Spectator Restaurant Award (Best of Award of Excellence 2024)
accolades: Best of Award of Excellence (Wine Spectator)

visits.wine: Turkish-spiced food with Rhône Valley red
visits.notes: Neighborhood gem with exceptional wine-food pairing

name: Che Fico
notes: Award of Excellence winner with 160 selections representing 1,500 bottles. Wine director Jason Alexander focuses heavily on Italian wines from Abruzzo to Sicily, including Roagna Barbaresco.
source: Wine Spectator Restaurant Award (Award of Excellence)

visits.notes: Rustic Italian atmosphere with generous wine portions

name: Ssal
notes: Best of Award of Excellence winner specializing in Korean fine dining. 550-label wine list with primary strengths in Burgundy and Champagne, challenging traditional Korean wine pairings.
source: Wine Spectator Restaurant Award (Best of Award of Excellence)
accolades: Best of Award of Excellence (Wine Spectator)

visits.wine: Burgundian Pinot Noir with Korean cuisine

name: Zuni Café
notes: Classic SF institution known for roast chicken for two. Traditional wine service with standard 5oz pours. One of the most consistent dining experiences in the city for over 30 years.
source: OpenTable 'vital SF restaurants', Reddit recommendations, local reputation

name: Kokkari Estiatorio
notes: Mediterranean restaurant with strong Greek wine program. Known for generous hospitality extending to wine service. Popular recommendation on local dining forums.
source: Local SF dining recommendations, Reddit r/sanfrancisco mentions

visits.notes: Mediterranean generosity extends to wine service

name: State Bird Provisions
notes: Michelin-starred restaurant with dim sum-style service. While food is exceptional, wine pours are noticeably smaller than expected for the price point. Mixed reviews on wine service.
source: Reddit dining discussions, OpenTable reviews, Michelin Guide (1 star)

visits.notes: Disappointing pour size for the price
